rulings.Case Title: PAMELA CANADA VS. ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurer to Complaint is OVERRULED in its entirety. At this stage of the case, the complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations or precluded by the prior settlements. The court cannot determine that as a matter of law that the settlement agreements bar claims arising from back-ups occurring after July, 2008. The statute of limitations does not necessarily and clearly bar every claim. Concealment is sufficiently alleged and is not uncertain. Cause of action for breach of contract sufficiently alleges legal effect of the lease. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAMELA CANADA VS. ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP, et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 2, 2013, line 5 - DEFENDANT ALL HALLOWS PRESERVATION LP's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's Motion to Strike is DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges facts re: malice and fraud. =(501/REQ)
